content:
# Decision: Redb VECTORS Table Over Custom Binary Sidecar (2026-03-27)

## Decision
Store embedding vectors in a dedicated Redb table with raw f32 bytes, not a custom binary sidecar file. Strip embeddings from JSON knowledge files.

## Why
- Custom sidecar requires ~500-1000 lines for crash safety, concurrency, compaction, alignment, mmap lifecycle
- Redb provides ACID transactions, MVCC, crash recovery out of the box — already a dependency
- Performance gap is negligible: Redb VECTORS rebuild ~0.3-0.5s vs sidecar ~0.1-0.2s at 100K entries
- 5-persona review identified 7 blocking issues with sidecar approach; Redb eliminates all of them
- Redb ENTRIES table already stores embeddings (as JSON bytes) — the data is already there

## How to Apply
- Add `VECTORS` Redb table: key = UUID bytes [u8; 16], value = raw f32 bytes [u8; 3072]
- Write vectors to VECTORS table inside existing `index_entry_into` write transaction
- Rebuild reads from VECTORS table via `bytemuck::cast_slice`, not JSON files
- Strip embeddings from JSON via `#[serde(skip_serializing)]` on `embedding` field
- Migration: two-phase — populate VECTORS table first, then strip JSON files
- Preserve old embedding-in-JSON read path for one major version (backward compat)

## Supersedes
Previous design from 2026-03-19 (`decisions/2026-03-19-json-embedding-exclusion-design.md`) proposed simply excluding embeddings from JSON with HNSW+Redb as cache. This decision adds the dedicated VECTORS table for optimal rebuild performance.
